   317→
   318→== Core Principles
   319→
   320→The Axial Lexicon provides reusable, atomic terminology for technical specifications.
   321→Each term maintains precise semantics while adapting to implementation context.
   322→Specifications selectively import only needed axial terms, avoiding vocabulary bloat.
   323→
   324→== Motif Vocabulary
   325→
   326→AXL terms are
   327→{axl_motif_s}
   328→- abstract patterns that concrete specifications voice in their local context.
   329→
   330→[[axl_motif]]
   331→{axl_motif}::
   332→An abstract pattern defined in the Axial Lexicon that can be voiced by concrete terms in specifications.
   333→A
   334→{axl_motif}
   335→captures the essential shape of a concept while remaining independent of implementation context.
   336→Specifications voice
   337→{axl_motif_s}
   338→through local terms that bind the abstract pattern to specific environments.
   339→
   340→[[axl_voicing]]
   341→{axl_voicing}::
   342→A concrete term in a specification that gives local expression to A
   343→{axl_motif}.
   344→Each
   345→{axl_voicing}
   346→adapts the abstract pattern to a specific context while preserving the
   347→{axl_motif_s}
   348→essential character.
   349→
   350→[[axl_voices]]
   351→{axl_voices}::
   352→The relationship where a concrete term expresses a
   353→{axl_motif}
   354→in a specific context.
   355→When a term
   356→{axl_voices}
   357→a
   358→{axl_motif},
   359→it gives the abstract pattern local form and implementation-specific behavior.
   360→
   361→[[axl_evokes]]
   362→{axl_evokes}::
   363→The relationship where A
   364→{axl_motif}
   365→calls forth its
   366→{axl_voicing_s}
   367→in the reader's understanding.
   368→When encountering A
   369→{axl_motif},
   370→it
   371→{axl_evokes}
   372→the concrete realizations known from various specifications.
   373→
   374→[[axl_annotation]]
   375→{axl_annotation}::
   376→A
   377→{xref_MCM}
   378→comment using Strachey brackets that declares the
   379→{axl_motif_s}
   380→a term
   381→{axl_voices}.
   382→The
   383→{axl_annotation}
   384→appears between anchor and definition as a space-separated list of AXL terms within Strachey brackets.
   385→+
   386→Format: `// ⟦relationship primary [dimension ...]⟧`
   387→+
   388→* First term: the relationship (typically `axl_voices`)
   389→* Second term: the primary
   390→{axl_motif}
   391→being voiced
   392→* Remaining terms: dimension terms (prefixed `axd_`) specifying characteristics
   393→+
   394→Examples:
   395→+
   396→* `// ⟦axl_voices axc_call⟧` - simple voicing
   397→* `// ⟦axl_voices axr_member axd_required axd_nullable⟧` - voicing with dimensions
   398→+
   399→Category prefixes carry semantic meaning, eliminating need for punctuation.
   400→Position determines role: relationship first, primary second, dimensions following.
   401→This design prioritizes LLM interpretability over human visual parsing.
   402→When ambiguity or friction arises during LLM processing, the format should evolve to resolve it.
   403→
   404→== Motif Implications
   405→
   406→{axl_motif_s} carry implied characteristics beyond their definitions.
   407→These implications guide how
   408→{axl_voicing_s}
   409→should behave:
   410→
   411→* Anchor placement expectations per
   412→{xref_MCM}
   413→form patterns
   414→* Type/instance level - where the
   415→{axl_motif}
   416→sits in abstraction hierarchy
   417→* Contextual requirements - parent structures or operations the
   418→{axl_motif}
   419→exists within
   420→* Voicing expectation - whether the
   421→{axl_motif}
   422→typically requires local voicing
   423→
   424→== Form Expectations
   425→
   426→Each AXL category implies an expected
   427→{xref_MCM}
   428→definition form pattern for documents using that category.
   429→During type-scheme linting, these expectations guide validation of definition structure.
   430→
   431→* {axc_*} control terms expect
   432→{xref_MCM}
   433→`mcm_form_deflist`
   434→* {axo_sequence} operations expect
   435→{xref_MCM}
   436→`mcm_form_section` with nested steps
   437→* {axj_structure} definitions expect
   438→{xref_MCM}
   439→`mcm_form_section`
   440→* {axj_field} definitions expect
   441→{xref_MCM}
   442→`mcm_form_bullet` under parent structure
   443→* {axk_premise} definitions expect
   444→{xref_MCM}
   445→`mcm_form_deflist`
   446→* {axs_*} section terms expect
   447→{xref_MCM}
   448→`mcm_form_section` for section headers
   449→* Most other categories expect
   450→{xref_MCM}
   451→`mcm_form_deflist`
   452→
   453→These expectations support the
   454→{axl_annotation}
   455→pattern where documents include `// ⟦axl_voices motif ...⟧` markers that are validated when AXL is in context.
   456→
   457→== Axial Control Terms
   458→
   459→[[axc_variable]]
   460→{axc_variable}::
   461→A
   462→{axl_motif}
   463→representing a temporary value holder denoted by distinctive delimiters that exists for the duration of an operational sequence.
   464→Implementation varies by context: shell variables in bash, dictionary entries in Python, sessionStorage in browser JavaScript.
   465→
   466→[[axc_fatal]]
   467→{axc_fatal}::
   468→A
   469→{axl_motif}
   470→representing immediate termination of the operational sequence with error status, preventing all subsequent steps from executing.
   471→Requires operator intervention or error recovery before retry.
   472→
   473→[[axc_warn]]
   474→{axc_warn}::
   475→A
   476→{axl_motif}
   477→representing display of a non-blocking warning message while allowing the operational sequence to continue execution.
   478→Alerts operators to concerning conditions without halting progress.
   479→
   480→[[axc_store]]
   481→{axc_store}::
   482→A
   483→{axl_motif}
   484→representing capture of a value from an operation result or computation into a
   485→{axc_variable}
   486→for later reference.
   487→Context determines storage mechanism while preserving semantic intent.
   488→
   489→[[axc_use]]
   490→{axc_use}::
   491→A
   492→{axl_motif}
   493→representing reference to a previously stored
   494→{axc_variable}
   495→value in the current operation.
   496→Retrieves stored values from context-appropriate storage location.
   497→
   498→[[axc_require]]
   499→{axc_require}::
   500→A
   501→{axl_motif}
   502→representing validation check that confirms an expected condition, triggering
   503→{axc_fatal}
   504→or alternate execution path if not met.
   505→Enforces preconditions and invariants throughout execution.
   506→
   507→[[axc_poll]]
   508→{axc_poll}::
   509→A
   510→{axl_motif}
   511→representing repeated execution of a status check at defined intervals until completion condition or timeout.
   512→Implements retry logic with configurable intervals and limits.
   513→
   514→[[axc_call]]
   515→{axc_call}::
   516→A
   517→{axl_motif}
   518→representing direct invocation of an external service, API endpoint, or command.
   519→Captures both success and error responses for processing.
   520→
   521→[[axc_submit]]
   522→{axc_submit}::
   523→A
   524→{axl_motif}
   525→representing initiation of an asynchronous operation that returns immediately with a handle for later completion tracking.
   526→Distinct from
   527→{axc_call}
   528→which expects synchronous response.
   529→
   530→[[axc_await]]
   531→{axc_await}::
   532→A
   533→{axl_motif}
   534→representing blocking wait on a submitted asynchronous operation until completion.
   535→Polls operation status until done, then returns final result or error.
   536→
   537→[[axc_show]]
   538→{axc_show}::
   539→A
   540→{axl_motif}
   541→representing presentation of operational facts or values to the operator.
   542→Implementation varies from console output to UI display based on context.
   543→
   544→[[axc_step]]
   545→{axc_step}::
   546→A
   547→{axl_motif}
   548→representing a normal progress message indicating successful completion of a processing stage.
   549→Provides operational visibility without implying warnings or errors.
   550→
   551→== Axial Environment Terms
   552→
   553→{axe_environment_s} specify execution contexts for control operations.
   554→{axl_voicing_s} of
   555→{axc_call},
   556→{axc_warn},
   557→{axc_fatal}
   558→and similar control motifs should include an appropriate
   559→{axe_environment}
   560→as a dimension to clarify how the control term manifests in that context.
   561→
   562→[[axe_environment]]
   563→{axe_environment}::
   564→A
   565→{axl_motif}
   566→representing an execution context that determines how control terms manifest.
   567→{axe_environment_s} appear as dimensions in
   568→{axl_voicing_s}
   569→to bind abstract control semantics to concrete operational behavior.
   570→
   571→[[axe_bash_interactive]]
   572→{axe_bash_interactive}::
   573→A
   574→{axl_motif}
   575→representing a bash shell session with human operator present at an interactive terminal.
   576→Feedback via stdout/stderr is immediate.
   577→Operator can interrupt (Ctrl-C) or respond to prompts.
   578→Warnings display as yellow text; fatals exit with non-zero status.
   579→
   580→[[axe_bash_unattended]]
   581→{axe_bash_unattended}::
   582→A
   583→{axl_motif}
   584→representing a bash script executing without human presence.
   585→Output captured to logs.
   586→No interactive prompts possible.
   587→Failures must be self-documenting for later analysis.
   588→
   589→[[axe_bash_scripted]]
   590→{axe_bash_scripted}::
   591→A
   592→{axl_motif}
   593→representing a bash script deliberately invoked to run to completion.
   594→No human interaction during execution.
   595→Succeeds atomically or fails with error status.
   596→Output may go to stdout/stderr for capture or display.
   597→Distinct from
   598→{axe_bash_unattended}
   599→which implies background/scheduled execution with log capture.
   600→
   601→[[axe_human_guide]]
   602→{axe_human_guide}::
   603→A
   604→{axl_motif}
   605→representing a human executing a specific rendered procedure document while interacting with an external system (web console, GUI application).
   606→The procedure document presents numbered steps; the human performs each in the external system.
   607→Feedback is visual confirmation in the external UI.
   608→Human judges success or failure of each step.
   609→Warnings are cautionary notes; fatals mean abort procedure.
   610→
   611→[[axe_rest_api]]
   612→{axe_rest_api}::
   613→A
   614→{axl_motif}
   615→representing invocation of an HTTP REST API endpoint.
   616→Request/response via JSON or other structured format.
   617→Authentication via bearer tokens, API keys, or similar.
   618→Calls return HTTP status codes and response bodies.
   619→
   620→[[axe_daemon_runtime]]
   621→{axe_daemon_runtime}::
   622→A
   623→{axl_motif}
   624→representing a long-running system process.
   625→Errors are logged, not displayed.
   626→Fatal means graceful shutdown with cleanup.
   627→No human in immediate feedback loop.
   628→
   629→== Axial Section Terms
   630→
   631→{axs_*} terms represent documentation sections for
   632→{axo_procedure}
   633→specifications.
   634→Each section motif captures a standard aspect of procedure documentation.
   635→{axl_voicing_s}
   636→adapt these abstract sections to interface-specific forms.
   637→
   638→[[axs_inputs]]
   639→{axs_inputs}::
   640→A
   641→{axl_motif}
   642→representing a section documenting what an
   643→{axo_procedure}
   644→requires to execute.
   645→Covers arguments, parameters, configuration, and input data.
   646→{axl_voicing_s}
   647→specify interface-appropriate input documentation:
   648→{axa_argument_list}
   649→for CLI,
   650→{axi_request_body}
   651→for REST APIs.
   652→
   653→[[axs_preconditions]]
   654→{axs_preconditions}::
   655→A
   656→{axl_motif}
   657→representing a section documenting environmental state requirements before
   658→{axo_procedure}
   659→execution.
   660→Optional section for procedures with significant prerequisites.
   661→Covers required services, authentication state, file system conditions, or resource availability.
   662→Distinct from
   663→{axs_inputs}
   664→which documents explicit parameters.
   665→
   666→[[axs_behavior]]
   667→{axs_behavior}::
   668→A
   669→{axl_motif}
   670→representing a section documenting what an
   671→{axo_procedure}
   672→does during execution.
   673→Describes the steps, actions, or transformations performed.
   674→{axl_voicing_s}
   675→may use
   676→{axo_sequence}
   677→patterns for ordered steps or prose for atomic operations.
   678→
   679→[[axs_outputs]]
   680→{axs_outputs}::
   681→A
   682→{axl_motif}
   683→representing a section documenting what an
   684→{axo_procedure}
   685→produces upon completion.
   686→Covers return values, created artifacts, modified state, or emitted data.
   687→{axl_voicing_s}
   688→specify interface-appropriate output documentation:
   689→{axi_response_body}
   690→for REST APIs, stdout/stderr patterns for CLI.
   691→
   692→[[axs_postconditions]]
   693→{axs_postconditions}::
   694→A
   695→{axl_motif}
   696→representing a section documenting environmental state guarantees after successful
   697→{axo_procedure}
   698→execution.
   699→Optional section for procedures with significant side effects.
   700→Covers created resources, modified permissions, or system state changes.
   701→Distinct from
   702→{axs_outputs}
   703→which documents explicit return values.
   704→
   705→[[axs_completion]]
   706→{axs_completion}::
   707→A
   708→{axl_motif}
   709→representing a section documenting how an
   710→{axo_procedure}
   711→signals success or failure.
   712→Covers exit codes, return status, completion indicators, or success criteria.
   713→{axl_voicing_s}
   714→specify interface-appropriate completion documentation:
   715→{axa_exit_uniform}
   716→or
   717→{axa_exit_enumerated}
   718→for CLI,
   719→HTTP status codes for REST APIs.
   720→
   721→[[axs_errors]]
   722→{axs_errors}::
   723→A
   724→{axl_motif}
   725→representing a section documenting failure modes of an
   726→{axo_procedure}.
   727→Optional section for procedures with non-obvious failure conditions.
   728→Covers error categories, recovery actions, or diagnostic guidance.
   729→Distinct from
   730→{axs_completion}
   731→which documents the signaling mechanism rather than failure semantics.
   732→
   733→=== Lifecycle Section Motifs
   734→
   735→These
   736→{axs_*}
   737→sections apply to specific lifecycle dimensions.
   738→
   739→[[axs_startup]]
   740→{axs_startup}::
   741→A
   742→{axl_motif}
   743→representing a section documenting initialization behavior for an
   744→{axd_longrunning}
   745→{axo_procedure}.
   746→Covers startup sequence, readiness criteria, and initialization dependencies.
   747→Required for
   748→{axd_longrunning}
   749→procedures.
   750→
   751→[[axs_shutdown]]
   752→{axs_shutdown}::
   753→A
   754→{axl_motif}
   755→representing a section documenting graceful termination for an
   756→{axd_longrunning}
   757→{axo_procedure}.
   758→Covers cleanup behavior, in-flight request handling, and resource release.
   759→Required for
   760→{axd_longrunning}
   761→procedures.
   762→
   763→[[axs_health]]
   764→{axs_health}::
   765→A
   766→{axl_motif}
   767→representing a section documenting health check semantics for an
   768→{axd_longrunning}
   769→{axo_procedure}.
   770→Covers liveness probes (is it running?), readiness probes (can it serve?), and health endpoints.
   771→Optional for
   772→{axd_longrunning}
   773→procedures.
   774→
   775→[[axs_signals]]
   776→{axs_signals}::
   777→A
   778→{axl_motif}
   779→representing a section documenting Unix signal handling for an
   780→{axo_procedure}.
   781→Covers SIGTERM (graceful stop), SIGHUP (reload config), SIGUSR1/2 (custom actions).
   782→Optional; most relevant for
   783→{axd_longrunning}
   784→but available to any
   785→{axo_command}.
   786→
   787→[[axs_recovery]]
   788→{axs_recovery}::
   789→A
   790→{axl_motif}
   791→representing a section documenting crash recovery behavior for an
   792→{axd_longrunning}
   793→{axo_procedure}.
   794→Covers restart behavior, state recovery, and failure mode handling.
   795→Optional for
   796→{axd_longrunning}
   797→procedures.
   798→
   799→[[axs_schedule]]
   800→{axs_schedule}::
   801→A
   802→{axl_motif}
   803→representing a section documenting timing specification for an
   804→{axd_periodic}
   805→{axo_procedure}.
   806→Covers cron expressions, intervals, or calendar-based triggers.
   807→Required for
   808→{axd_periodic}
   809→procedures.
   810→
   811→[[axs_idempotency]]
   812→{axs_idempotency}::
   813→A
   814→{axl_motif}
   815→representing a section documenting re-run safety for an
   816→{axd_periodic}
   817→{axo_procedure}.
   818→Covers duplicate handling, safe re-execution, and state management across runs.
   819→Optional for
   820→{axd_periodic}
   821→procedures.
   822→
   823→[[axs_overlap]]
   824→{axs_overlap}::
   825→A
   826→{axl_motif}
   827→representing a section documenting concurrent execution behavior for an
   828→{axd_periodic}
   829→{axo_procedure}.
   830→Covers what happens when a new run triggers while previous run is still executing.
   831→Options include: skip, queue, terminate previous, or allow parallel.
   832→Optional for
   833→{axd_periodic}
   834→procedures.
   835→
   836→== Axial Operation Terms
   837→
   838→=== Procedure Hierarchy
   839→
   840→{axo_procedure}
   841→is the superset
   842→{axl_motif}
   843→for procedural articulations.
   844→{axo_command},
   845→{axo_guide},
   846→and
   847→{axo_routine}
   848→are specialized forms that voice
   849→{axo_procedure}
   850→with additional constraints.
   851→
   852→{axo_sequence}
   853→is a structural characteristic describing HOW behavior is organized, not a procedure type.
   854→Most procedures ARE sequential.
   855→
   856→The following
   857→{axo_*}
   858→terms are NOT procedures and remain outside the hierarchy:
   859→{axo_entity},
   860→{axo_role},
   861→{axo_identity},
   862→{axo_actor},
   863→{axo_dependency}.
   864→
   865→[[axo_procedure]]
   866→{axo_procedure}::
   867→A
   868→{axl_motif}
   869→representing an executable operation with defined inputs, behavior, outputs, and completion semantics.
   870→{axo_procedure}
   871→is the superset for all procedural articulations.
   872→{axl_voicing_s}
   873→specialize by executor (machine vs human), reusability (terminal vs composite), and interface (CLI, REST, console).
   874→Documentation follows
   875→{axs_*}
   876→section patterns.
   877→
   878→[[axo_command]]
   879→{axo_command}::
   880→A
   881→{axl_motif}
   882→representing an
   883→{axo_procedure}
   884→executed by machine without human intervention.
   885→{axl_voices}
   886→{axo_procedure}
   887→with machine executor constraint.
   888→Requires
   889→{axe_environment}
   890→dimension specifying execution context.
   891→
   892→[[axo_guide]]
   893→{axo_guide}::
   894→A
   895→{axl_motif}
   896→representing an
   897→{axo_procedure}
   898→executed by a human operator following system-presented steps.
   899→{axl_voices}
   900→{axo_procedure}
   901→with human executor constraint.
   902→The system displays steps; the human performs them in external systems or consoles.
   903→Behavior steps use
   904→{axe_human_guide}
   905→control voicings.
   906→
   907→[[axo_routine]]
   908→{axo_routine}::
   909→A
   910→{axl_motif}
   911→representing a reusable
   912→{axo_procedure}
   913→invoked as a subsequence within other procedures.
   914→{axl_voices}
   915→{axo_procedure}
   916→with composite reusability constraint.
   917→Encapsulates common logic for consistent reuse across specifications.
   918→
   919→[[axo_sequence]]
   920→{axo_sequence}::
   921→A
   922→{axl_motif}
   923→representing a structural characteristic: ordered processing steps executed in defined progression.
   924→NOT a procedure type—describes HOW behavior is organized, not WHAT kind of procedure something is.
   925→Most
   926→{axo_procedure}
   927→voicings ARE sequential.
   928→Use within
   929→{axs_behavior}
   930→sections to document step ordering.
   931→
   932→[[axo_role]]
   933→{axo_role}::
   934→A
   935→{axl_motif}
   936→representing an operational identity pattern that defines capabilities and permissions.
   937→Determines available operations and access boundaries.
   938→
   939→[[axo_identity]]
   940→{axo_identity}::
   941→A
   942→{axl_motif}
   943→representing the abstract concept of an operational identity.
   944→An entity capable of authentication and holding authorities within a system.
   945→{axo_identity} is the pattern;
   946→{axo_actor_s}
   947→are named instantiations with specific bindings.
   948→
   949→[[axo_actor]]
   950→{axo_actor}::
   951→A
   952→{axl_motif}
   953→representing a specific named identity with defined responsibilities and exclusive authority over an operational domain.
   954→{axo_actor_s} instantiate
   955→{axo_identity}
   956→with concrete bindings (human via OAuth, service via service account) and permissions.
   957→
   958→[[axo_entity]]
   959→{axo_entity}::
   960→A
   961→{axl_motif}
   962→representing an object with both structure and behavior.
   963→Unlike
   964→{axr_record}
   965→which captures pure data,
   966→{axo_entity}
   967→includes methods, builder patterns, or lifecycle operations.
   968→{axl_voicing_s}
   969→describe the entity's data members and its behavioral interface (construction, mutation, registration).
   970→Typical manifestations include Rust structs with `impl` blocks, builder-pattern APIs, or objects that participate in registration protocols.
   971→
   972→== Axial Type Terms
   973→
   974→[[axt_type]]
   975→{axt_type}::
   976→A
   977→{axl_motif}
   978→representing a data format constraint that defines valid values and structures.
   979→Enforces data validation and enables type-safe operations.
   980→
   981→[[axt_string]]
   982→{axt_string}::
   983→A
   984→{axl_motif}
   985→representing text data type with optional pattern validation.
   986→{axl_voicing_s} may address context-specific concerns including:
   987→+
   988→* Encoding (ASCII, UTF-8, locale-dependent character sets)
   989→* Termination semantics (null-terminated, length-prefixed, delimiter-bounded)
   990→* Mutability (immutable values, mutable buffers)
   991→* Memory management (owned, borrowed, reference-counted)
   992→* Safety constraints (shell-safe, URL-safe, JSON-safe, SQL-safe)
   993→* Format patterns (timestamps, identifiers, hashes, paths)
   994→* Size and bounds (fixed-length, maximum length, unbounded)
   995→
   996→[[axt_number]]
   997→{axt_number}::
   998→A
   999→{axl_motif}
  1000→representing numeric value type supporting integers and floating point.
  1001→May specify range constraints or precision requirements.
  1002→
  1003→[[axt_boolean]]
  1004→{axt_boolean}::
  1005→A
  1006→{axl_motif}
  1007→representing binary true/false value type.
  1008→Maps to language-specific boolean implementations.
  1009→
  1010→[[axt_array]]
  1011→{axt_array}::
  1012→A
  1013→{axl_motif}
  1014→representing ordered collection of typed elements.
  1015→Specifies element type and optional size constraints.
  1016→
  1017→[[axt_object]]
  1018→{axt_object}::
  1019→A
  1020→{axl_motif}
  1021→representing named field collection forming a composite type.
  1022→Contains typed fields with defined cardinality.
  1023→
  1024→[[axt_enumeration]]
  1025→{axt_enumeration}::
  1026→A
  1027→{axl_motif}
  1028→representing constrained set of valid string values.
  1029→Restricts values to predefined options.
  1030→
  1031→[[axt_enum_value]]
  1032→{axt_enum_value}::
  1033→A
  1034→{axl_motif}
  1035→representing single allowed option within an
  1036→{axt_enumeration}.
  1037→Must be explicitly listed in enumeration definition.
  1038→
  1039→[[axt_variant]]
  1040→{axt_variant}::
  1041→A
  1042→{axl_motif}
  1043→representing a value that can take one of several distinct forms, where each form may have different structure.
  1044→Unlike
  1045→{axt_enumeration}
  1046→which constrains to predefined strings,
  1047→{axt_variant}
  1048→allows structurally different alternatives.
  1049→{axl_voicing_s} define the specific forms and discrimination logic for their context.
  1050→
  1051→== Axial Record Terms
  1052→
  1053→{axr_record_s} and
  1054→{axr_member_s}
  1055→provide format-agnostic patterns for structured data.
  1056→Format-specific variants (JSON, protobuf, XML, etc.) capture serialization-specific semantics.
  1057→{axr_member_s} inherit format context from their parent
  1058→{axr_record}.
  1059→
  1060→[[axr_record]]
  1061→{axr_record}::
  1062→A
  1063→{axl_motif}
  1064→representing a named collection of typed
  1065→{axr_member_s}.
  1066→Abstract pattern that format-specific variants voice with serialization semantics.
  1067→Use format-specific variants (
  1068→{axr_record_json},
  1069→{axr_record_protobuf},
  1070→etc.) when format is known.
  1071→
  1072→[[axr_record_json]]
  1073→{axr_record_json}::
  1074→A
  1075→{axl_motif}
  1076→representing a JSON object structure.
  1077→{axr_member_s} are unordered, string-keyed properties.
  1078→Requires
  1079→{axd_nullable}
  1080→specification since JSON has explicit null.
  1081→
  1082→[[axr_record_protobuf]]
  1083→{axr_record_protobuf}::
  1084→A
  1085→{axl_motif}
  1086→representing a protobuf message.
  1087→{axr_member_s} require
  1088→{axd_field_number}
  1089→and
  1090→{axd_wire_type}
  1091→dimensions.
  1092→Supports schema evolution through field numbering.
  1093→
  1094→[[axr_record_xml]]
  1095→{axr_record_xml}::
  1096→A
  1097→{axl_motif}
  1098→representing an XML element structure.
  1099→{axr_member_s} require
  1100→{axd_xml_form}
  1101→to distinguish attributes from child elements.
  1102→Order may be significant depending on schema.
  1103→
  1104→[[axr_record_yaml]]
  1105→{axr_record_yaml}::
  1106→A
  1107→{axl_motif}
  1108→representing a YAML mapping.
  1109→Superset of JSON with anchors, aliases, and multi-document support.
  1110→{axr_member_s} follow JSON-like semantics with extended syntax.
  1111→
  1112→[[axr_record_binary]]
  1113→{axr_record_binary}::
  1114→A
  1115→{axl_motif}
  1116→representing a binary/memory structure.
  1117→{axr_member_s} require
  1118→{axd_endianness}
  1119→for multi-byte fields.
  1120→Fixed layout with explicit size and alignment.
  1121→
  1122→[[axr_member]]
  1123→{axr_member}::
  1124→A
  1125→{axl_motif}
  1126→representing a named, typed element within an
  1127→{axr_record}.
  1128→Inherits serialization format from parent
  1129→{axr_record}.
  1130→Qualified by
  1131→{axd_dimension_s}
  1132→in
  1133→{axl_annotation}:
  1134→`// ⟦axl_voices axr_member axd_required axd_nullable⟧`
  1135→
  1136→== Axial JSON Terms
  1137→
  1138→[[axj_structure]]
  1139→{axj_structure}::
  1140→A
  1141→{axl_motif}
  1142→representing named schema definition for JSON objects.
  1143→Defines complete object shape with field specifications.
  1144→
  1145→[[axj_field]]
  1146→{axj_field}::
  1147→A
  1148→{axl_motif}
  1149→representing named, typed element within a
  1150→{axj_structure}.
  1151→Combines tag identifier with type constraint.
  1152→
  1153→[[axj_field_tag]]
  1154→{axj_field_tag}::
  1155→A
  1156→{axl_motif}
  1157→representing the key identifier for a
  1158→{axj_field}.
  1159→Follows naming conventions of target specification.
  1160→
  1161→[[axj_field_type]]
  1162→{axj_field_type}::
  1163→A
  1164→{axl_motif}
  1165→representing the value constraint for a
  1166→{axj_field}.
  1167→Determines parsing and validation behavior.
  1168→
  1169→[[axj_array_element]]
  1170→{axj_array_element}::
  1171→A
  1172→{axl_motif}
  1173→representing type constraint for members of an
  1174→{axt_array}
  1175→field.
  1176→Applies uniformly to all array elements.
  1177→
  1178→[[axj_nested_field]]
  1179→{axj_nested_field}::
  1180→A
  1181→{axl_motif}
  1182→representing fields within object-typed fields enabling hierarchy.
  1183→Maintains type safety through nested validation.
  1184→
  1185→[[axj_root_structure]]
  1186→{axj_root_structure}::
  1187→A
  1188→{axl_motif}
  1189→representing top-level JSON object schema.
  1190→Entry point for JSON document validation.
  1191→
  1192→[[axj_field_cardinality]]
  1193→{axj_field_cardinality}::
  1194→A
  1195→{axl_motif}
  1196→representing required, optional, or repeated nature of a field.
  1197→Maps to validation rules in implementation.
  1198→
  1199→[[axj_type_reference]]
  1200→{axj_type_reference}::
  1201→A
  1202→{axl_motif}
  1203→representing pointer to another
  1204→{axj_structure}
  1205→for composition.
  1206→Maintains referential integrity across definitions.
  1207→
  1208→== Axial Argument Terms
  1209→
  1210→[[axa_argument]]
  1211→{axa_argument}::
  1212→A
  1213→{axl_motif}
  1214→representing command parameter accepting a value.
  1215→Foundation for CLI and function interfaces.
  1216→
  1217→[[axa_positional]]
  1218→{axa_positional}::
  1219→A
  1220→{axl_motif}
  1221→representing order-determines-meaning parameter in command invocation.
  1222→Position in argument list defines semantic role.
  1223→
  1224→[[axa_keyword]]
  1225→{axa_keyword}::
  1226→A
  1227→{axl_motif}
  1228→representing name-determines-meaning parameter with explicit identifier.
  1229→Order-independent with explicit naming.
  1230→
  1231→[[axa_long_option]]
  1232→{axa_long_option}::
  1233→A
  1234→{axl_motif}
  1235→representing keyword argument with `--` prefix following GNU conventions.
  1236→Uses hyphenated multi-word names.
  1237→
  1238→[[axa_short_option]]
  1239→{axa_short_option}::
  1240→A
  1241→{axl_motif}
  1242→representing keyword argument with single `-` prefix for brevity.
  1243→Often provides shorthand for
  1244→{axa_long_option}.
  1245→
  1246→[[axa_option_name]]
  1247→{axa_option_name}::
  1248→A
  1249→{axl_motif}
  1250→representing the identifier portion of a keyword argument.
  1251→Defines the semantic meaning of the option.
  1252→
  1253→[[axa_option_value]]
  1254→{axa_option_value}::
  1255→A
  1256→{axl_motif}
  1257→representing the data portion following an option identifier.
  1258→Subject to
  1259→{axa_argument_type}
  1260→constraints.
  1261→
  1262→[[axa_argument_type]]
  1263→{axa_argument_type}::
  1264→A
  1265→{axl_motif}
  1266→representing expected data format for an
  1267→{axa_argument}.
  1268→Enforces type safety at interface boundary.
  1269→
  1270→[[axa_required]]
  1271→{axa_required}::
  1272→A
  1273→{axl_motif}
  1274→representing attribute indicating an
  1275→{axa_argument}
  1276→must be provided.
  1277→Absence triggers
  1278→{axc_fatal}
  1279→or error response.
  1280→
  1281→[[axa_optional]]
  1282→{axa_optional}::
  1283→A
  1284→{axl_motif}
  1285→representing attribute indicating an
  1286→{axa_argument}
  1287→may be omitted.
  1288→Provides flexibility in interface usage.
  1289→
  1290→[[axa_defaulted]]
  1291→{axa_defaulted}::
  1292→A
  1293→{axl_motif}
  1294→representing attribute indicating an
  1295→{axa_argument}
  1296→has a fallback value.
  1297→Specifies behavior when
  1298→{axa_optional}
  1299→argument omitted.
  1300→
  1301→[[axa_argument_list]]
  1302→{axa_argument_list}::
  1303→A
  1304→{axl_motif}
  1305→representing a structured section documenting an operation's arguments.
  1306→{axl_voices}
  1307→{axs_inputs}
  1308→in CLI context.
  1309→Within an
  1310→{axa_argument_list},
  1311→{axd_dimension}
  1312→annotations may precede bullet items to qualify argument usage without requiring anchors.
  1313→Each item references a globally-defined argument term.
  1314→The voicing is placed on the section header term definition, not repeated at each usage.
  1315→+
  1316→Example section header definition:
  1317→+
  1318→[source,asciidoc]
  1319→----
  1320→[[jjds_arguments]]
  1321→// ⟦axl_voices axa_argument_list⟧
  1322→{jjds_arguments}::
  1323→Section header introducing operation arguments.
  1324→----
  1325→+
  1326→Example usage in an operation:
  1327→+
  1328→[source,asciidoc]
  1329→----
  1330→{jjds_arguments}
  1331→
  1332→// ⟦axd_required⟧
  1333→* {jjda_silks}
  1334→
  1335→// ⟦axd_optional axd_defaulted⟧
  1336→* {jjda_file}
  1337→----
  1338→
  1339→[[axa_cli_option]]
  1340→{axa_cli_option}::
  1341→A
  1342→{axl_motif}
  1343→representing a command-line option definition.
  1344→{axl_voicing_s}
  1345→require the definition text to specify:
  1346→+
  1347→* The long form (e.g., `--file`)
  1348→* The short form, or its absence (e.g., `-f` or "none")
  1349→+
  1350→Example:
  1351→+
  1352→[source,asciidoc]
  1353→----
  1354→[[jjda_file]]
  1355→// ⟦axl_voices axa_cli_option⟧
  1356→{jjda_file}::
  1357→Path to the Gallops JSON file.
  1358→Long: `--file`.
  1359→Short: none.
  1360→Default: `.claude/jjm/jjg_gallops.json`.
  1361→----
  1362→
  1363→[[axa_exit_uniform]]
  1364→{axa_exit_uniform}::
  1365→A
  1366→{axl_motif}
  1367→representing an Exit Status section that follows standard Unix conventions.
  1368→{axl_voices}
  1369→{axs_completion}
  1370→in CLI context.
  1371→Exit 0 indicates success; any non-zero exit indicates error.
  1372→{axl_voicing_s}
  1373→may use terse notation since the pattern is conventional.
  1374→The voicing is placed on the section header term definition, not repeated at each usage.
  1375→Specifications define separate section header terms for uniform vs enumerated exit patterns.
  1376→+
  1377→Example section header definition:
  1378→+
  1379→[source,asciidoc]
  1380→----
  1381→[[jjds_exit_uniform]]
  1382→// ⟦axl_voices axa_exit_uniform⟧
  1383→{jjds_exit_uniform}::
  1384→Section header for operations with standard exit semantics.
  1385→----
  1386→+
  1387→Example usage:
  1388→+
  1389→[source,asciidoc]
  1390→----
  1391→{jjds_exit_uniform} 0 success, non-zero error.
  1392→----
  1393→
  1394→[[axa_exit_enumerated]]
  1395→{axa_exit_enumerated}::
  1396→A
  1397→{axl_motif}
  1398→representing an Exit Status section with distinct exit codes carrying specific meanings.
  1399→{axl_voices}
  1400→{axs_completion}
  1401→in CLI context.
  1402→{axl_voicing_s}
  1403→require the definition text to list each exit code and its meaning.
  1404→The voicing is placed on the section header term definition, not repeated at each usage.
  1405→Specifications define separate section header terms for uniform vs enumerated exit patterns.
  1406→+
  1407→Example section header definition:
  1408→+
  1409→[source,asciidoc]
  1410→----
  1411→[[jjds_exit_enumerated]]
  1412→// ⟦axl_voices axa_exit_enumerated⟧
  1413→{jjds_exit_enumerated}::
  1414→Section header for operations with distinct exit codes.
  1415→----
  1416→+
  1417→Example usage:
  1418→+
  1419→[source,asciidoc]
  1420→----
  1421→{jjds_exit_enumerated}
  1422→
  1423→* 0 — found and valid
  1424→* 1 — not found
  1425→* 2 — found but malformed
  1426→----
  1427→
  1428→== Axial Interface Terms
  1429→
  1430→[[axi_command_interface]]
  1431→{axi_command_interface}::
  1432→A
  1433→{axl_motif}
  1434→representing complete specification of a command's invocation pattern.
  1435→Defines syntax and semantics for command usage.
  1436→
  1437→[[axi_subcommand]]
  1438→{axi_subcommand}::
  1439→A
  1440→{axl_motif}
  1441→representing nested command within parent command structure.
  1442→Inherits context from parent command.
  1443→
  1444→[[axi_command_signature]]
  1445→{axi_command_signature}::
  1446→A
  1447→{axl_motif}
  1448→representing complete parameter specification for a command.
  1449→Serves as contract for command invocation.
  1450→
  1451→[[axi_endpoint]]
  1452→{axi_endpoint}::
  1453→A
  1454→{axl_motif}
  1455→representing URL path accepting HTTP requests.
  1456→Defines resource location and accepted operations.
  1457→
  1458→[[axi_api_method]]
  1459→{axi_api_method}::
  1460→A
  1461→{axl_motif}
  1462→representing HTTP verb combined with
  1463→{axi_endpoint}
  1464→specification.
  1465→Includes request and response specifications.
  1466→
  1467→[[axi_url_parameter]]
  1468→{axi_url_parameter}::
  1469→A
  1470→{axl_motif}
  1471→representing query string key-value pair in URL.
  1472→Appears after `?` in URL structure.
  1473→
  1474→[[axi_hash_parameter]]
  1475→{axi_hash_parameter}::
  1476→A
  1477→{axl_motif}
  1478→representing fragment identifier for client-side state.
  1479→Persists state without server round-trips.
  1480→
  1481→[[axi_request_body]]
  1482→{axi_request_body}::
  1483→A
  1484→{axl_motif}
  1485→representing structured input payload for HTTP requests.
  1486→Validated against
  1487→{axj_structure}
  1488→definitions.
  1489→
  1490→[[axi_response_body]]
  1491→{axi_response_body}::
  1492→A
  1493→{axl_motif}
  1494→representing structured output payload from HTTP responses.
  1495→Conforms to documented
  1496→{axj_structure}.
  1497→
  1498→[[axi_cli_interface]]
  1499→{axi_cli_interface}::
  1500→A
  1501→{axl_motif}
  1502→representing command-line invocation pattern specification.
  1503→Documents
  1504→{axa_argument_s}
  1505→and behavior.
  1506→
  1507→[[axi_cli_program]]
  1508→{axi_cli_program}::
  1509→A
  1510→{axl_motif}
  1511→representing a Unix-style command-line program.
  1512→Invoked from a shell with arguments.
  1513→Writes results to stdout, errors to stderr.
  1514→Returns exit status: 0 for success, non-zero for failure.
  1515→Suitable for scripted, non-interactive use.
  1516→
  1517→[[axi_cli_command_group]]
  1518→{axi_cli_command_group}::
  1519→A
  1520→{axl_motif}
  1521→representing a namespace within a CLI that contains subcommands.
  1522→Does not perform operations itself; serves as organizational container.
  1523→May be nested within an
  1524→{axi_cli_program}
  1525→or another
  1526→{axi_cli_command_group}.
  1527→Inherits Unix CLI semantics from parent.
  1528→
  1529→[[axi_cli_subcommand]]
  1530→{axi_cli_subcommand}::
  1531→A
  1532→{axl_motif}
  1533→representing a leaf operation within an
  1534→{axi_cli_program}
  1535→or
  1536→{axi_cli_command_group}.
  1537→Performs an actual operation; not a container for other commands.
  1538→Inherits Unix CLI semantics from parent: arguments, stdout, stderr, exit status.
  1539→
  1540→[[axi_rest_api]]
  1541→{axi_rest_api}::
  1542→A
  1543→{axl_motif}
  1544→representing resource-based web service interface.
  1545→Maps operations to HTTP methods and resources.
  1546→
  1547→[[axi_rpc_interface]]
  1548→{axi_rpc_interface}::
  1549→A
  1550→{axl_motif}
  1551→representing remote procedure call pattern for service invocation.
  1552→Emphasizes operation semantics over HTTP methods.
  1553→
  1554→[[axi_sse_endpoint]]
  1555→{axi_sse_endpoint}::
  1556→A
  1557→{axl_motif}
  1558→representing server-sent event stream for push notifications.
  1559→Enables real-time updates to connected clients.
  1560→
  1561→[[axi_cc_slash_command]]
  1562→{axi_cc_slash_command}::
  1563→A
  1564→{axl_motif}
  1565→representing a Claude Code slash command skill artifact.
  1566→Implemented as a `.claude/commands/*.md` file containing orchestration logic that the LLM executes as a skill.
  1567→Slash commands coordinate multiple
  1568→{axi_cli_subcommand}
  1569→invocations, gather context, and apply workflow policies.
  1570→
  1571→[[axi_cc_claudemd_verb]]
  1572→{axi_cc_claudemd_verb}::
  1573→A
  1574→{axl_motif}
  1575→representing an action verb defined in a
  1576→{axi_cc_claudemd_section}
  1577→that maps user intent to tool-layer operations.
  1578→The verb exists as a concept in the LLM context — either mapping directly to a single
  1579→{axi_cli_subcommand}
  1580→(direct verb) or orchestrating multiple operations (multistep verb implemented by a
  1581→{axi_cc_slash_command}).
  1582→
  1583→[[axi_cc_claudemd_section]]
  1584→{axi_cc_claudemd_section}::
  1585→A
  1586→{axl_motif}
  1587→representing a managed section within a project CLAUDE.md file.
  1588→Provides persistent LLM context that bridges specification-layer concepts to runtime execution.
  1589→Typically contains command references, vocabulary mappings, and operational guidance.
  1590→
  1591→== Axial Dimension Terms
  1592→
  1593→{axd_dimension_s} are modifiers that appear in
  1594→{axl_annotation_s}
  1595→to specify characteristics of members and arguments.
  1596→Unlike primary
  1597→{axl_motif_s},
  1598→{axd_dimension_s}
  1599→do not stand alone but qualify how a term voices its primary motif.
  1600→Applicability of
  1601→{axd_dimension_s}
  1602→depends on serialization format context.
  1603→
  1604→[[axd_dimension]]
  1605→{axd_dimension}::
  1606→A characteristic modifier that qualifies a member or argument in an
  1607→{axl_annotation}.
  1608→{axd_dimension_s} appear after the primary
  1609→{axl_motif}
  1610→in annotation format: `// ⟦axl_voices primary dimension ...⟧`
  1611→
  1612→=== Value Dimensions
  1613→
  1614→These
  1615→{axd_dimension_s}
  1616→describe value characteristics and apply across most serialization formats.
  1617→They encompass both presence semantics (required, optional, defaulted) and structural semantics (repeated, list).
  1618→
  1619→[[axd_required]]
  1620→{axd_required}::
  1621→Member or argument must be present.
  1622→Absence triggers validation error or
  1623→{axc_fatal}.
  1624→
  1625→[[axd_optional]]
  1626→{axd_optional}::
  1627→Member or argument may be omitted.
  1628→Absence is valid and handled gracefully.
  1629→
  1630→[[axd_nullable]]
  1631→{axd_nullable}::
  1632→Null, nil, or None is a valid value, distinct from absent.
  1633→Required for JSON where null is an explicit value type.
  1634→Some formats (protobuf) conflate nullable with optional.
  1635→
  1636→[[axd_none]]
  1637→{axd_none}::
  1638→Section or field is explicitly declared as having no content.
  1639→Distinguishes intentional absence from omission.
  1640→Use in section annotations to mark compliance with required sections that have no applicable content.
  1641→
  1642→[[axd_defaulted]]
  1643→{axd_defaulted}::
  1644→Has fallback value when absent.
  1645→Voicing should specify the default value in prose.
  1646→
  1647→[[axd_repeated]]
  1648→{axd_repeated}::
  1649→May have multiple values (array, list, repeated field).
  1650→Absence typically means empty collection, not error.
  1651→Applies to usage sites (arguments, members) where multiple occurrences are permitted.
  1652→
  1653→[[axd_list]]
  1654→{axd_list}::
  1655→Single value containing delimited multiple values.
  1656→Distinct from
  1657→{axd_repeated}
  1658→which indicates multiple separate occurrences.
  1659→Applies to type voicings where the type intrinsically represents a delimited collection.
  1660→Voicing should specify the delimiter in prose (space-delimited, comma-delimited, etc.).
  1661→
  1662→=== Format-Specific Dimensions
  1663→
  1664→These
  1665→{axd_dimension_s}
  1666→apply only to specific serialization formats.
  1667→Using them in forbidden contexts is a type-scheme error.
  1668→
  1669→[[axd_field_number]]
  1670→{axd_field_number}::
  1671→Numeric field identifier for protobuf encoding.
  1672→Required for protobuf format.
  1673→Forbidden for JSON, XML, YAML, binary.
  1674→
  1675→[[axd_wire_type]]
  1676→{axd_wire_type}::
  1677→Protobuf encoding type (varint, fixed32, fixed64, length-delimited).
  1678→Required for protobuf format.
  1679→Forbidden for JSON, XML, YAML, binary.
  1680→
  1681→[[axd_endianness]]
  1682→{axd_endianness}::
  1683→Byte order for binary encoding (big-endian, little-endian).
  1684→Required for binary format.
  1685→Forbidden for JSON, XML, YAML, protobuf.
  1686→
  1687→[[axd_xml_form]]
  1688→{axd_xml_form}::
  1689→Whether XML member is attribute or child element.
  1690→Required for XML format.
  1691→Forbidden for JSON, YAML, protobuf, binary.
  1692→
  1693→=== Lifecycle Dimensions
  1694→
  1695→These
  1696→{axd_dimension_s}
  1697→qualify
  1698→{axo_procedure}
  1699→voicings to specify execution lifecycle.
  1700→Exactly one lifecycle dimension is required for any
  1701→{axo_procedure}
  1702→voicing.
  1703→Lifecycle dimensions are mutually exclusive.
  1704→
  1705→[[axd_transient]]
  1706→{axd_transient}::
  1707→{axo_procedure}
  1708→executes and completes.
  1709→The default lifecycle for most procedures.
  1710→{axs_completion}
  1711→documents exit status or return value.
  1712→
  1713→[[axd_longrunning]]
  1714→{axd_longrunning}::
  1715→{axo_procedure}
  1716→runs continuously until explicitly stopped.
  1717→{axs_completion}
  1718→documents startup success criteria, not termination.
  1719→Requires
  1720→{axs_startup}
  1721→and
  1722→{axs_shutdown}
  1723→sections.
  1724→May include
  1725→{axs_health},
  1726→{axs_signals},
  1727→{axs_recovery}
  1728→sections.
  1729→
  1730→[[axd_periodic]]
  1731→{axd_periodic}::
  1732→{axo_procedure}
  1733→executes on a schedule.
  1734→{axs_completion}
  1735→applies per-run.
  1736→Requires
  1737→{axs_schedule}
  1738→section.
  1739→May include
  1740→{axs_idempotency},
  1741→{axs_overlap}
  1742→sections.
  1743→
  1744→=== Regime Dimensions
  1745→
  1746→These
  1747→{axd_dimension_s}
  1748→qualify regime structure annotations.
  1749→
  1750→[[axd_conditional]]
  1751→{axd_conditional}::
  1752→Group activation depends on one or more gate conditions.
  1753→A group annotated with
  1754→{axd_conditional}
  1755→requires corresponding hierarchy markers in the subdocument to include at least one
  1756→{axhrgc_gate}
  1757→specifying the activation criteria.
  1758→
  1759→=== Dimension Applicability
  1760→
  1761→Per-format guidance for
  1762→{axd_dimension}
  1763→usage:
  1764→
  1765→[cols="<,^,^,^,^,^",options="header"]
  1766→|===
  1767→|Dimension |JSON |Protobuf |XML |Binary |YAML
  1768→
  1769→|{axd_required}     |recommend |recommend |recommend |recommend |recommend
  1770→|{axd_optional}     |recommend |recommend |recommend |recommend |recommend
  1771→|{axd_nullable}     |required  |implicit  |recommend |context   |recommend
  1772→|{axd_defaulted}    |optional  |required  |optional  |optional  |optional
  1773→|{axd_repeated}     |recommend |required  |recommend |recommend |recommend
  1774→|{axd_list}         |optional  |optional  |optional  |optional  |optional
  1775→|{axd_field_number} |forbidden |required  |forbidden |forbidden |forbidden
  1776→|{axd_wire_type}    |forbidden |required  |forbidden |forbidden |forbidden
  1777→|{axd_endianness}   |forbidden |forbidden |forbidden |required  |forbidden
  1778→|{axd_xml_form}     |forbidden |forbidden |required  |forbidden |forbidden
  1779→|===
  1780→
  1781→Legend: required = must specify; recommend = should specify; optional = may specify; implicit = format handles; context = depends on usage; forbidden = not applicable.
  1782→
  1783→== Axial Format Terms
  1784→
  1785→{axf_format_s} specify how configuration data is serialized and carried.
  1786→{axrg_assignment_s} use a particular
  1787→{axf_format}
  1788→to encode
  1789→{axrg_variable}
  1790→values in files that can be sourced, included, or parsed.
  1791→
  1792→[[axf_format]]
  1793→{axf_format}::
  1794→A
  1795→{axl_motif}
  1796→representing a serialization encoding for configuration data.
  1797→{axf_format_s} determine how
  1798→{axrg_assignment_s}
  1799→are written to and read from files.
  1800→
  1801→[[axf_bash]]
  1802→{axf_bash}::
  1803→A
  1804→{axl_motif}
  1805→representing bash script format for configuration.
  1806→Files are source-able via `. file` or `source file`.
  1807→Variables declared as `VAR=value` without export, or `export VAR=value` for environment propagation.
  1808→
  1809→[[axf_makefile]]
  1810→{axf_makefile}::
  1811→A
  1812→{axl_motif}
  1813→representing makefile format for configuration.
  1814→Files are includable via `include file`.
  1815→Variables declared as `VAR := value` for immediate expansion or `VAR = value` for deferred expansion.
  1816→
  1817→[[axf_json]]
  1818→{axf_json}::
  1819→A
  1820→{axl_motif}
  1821→representing JSON format for configuration.
  1822→Standard JSON object structure with string keys mapping to typed values.
  1823→Parsed by standard JSON libraries.
  1824→
  1825→[[axf_yaml]]
  1826→{axf_yaml}::
  1827→A
  1828→{axl_motif}
  1829→representing YAML format for configuration.
  1830→Superset of JSON with additional syntax for readability.
  1831→Parsed by YAML libraries with attention to version-specific semantics.
  1832→
  1833→== Axial Regime Terms
  1834→
  1835→{axrg_regime_s} are structured configuration systems that coordinate specification, validation, and rendering of typed configuration data.
  1836→
  1837→[[axrg_regime]]
  1838→{axrg_regime}::
  1839→A
  1840→{axl_motif}
  1841→representing a structured configuration system.
  1842→A
  1843→{axrg_regime}
  1844→binds related
  1845→{axrg_variable_s}
  1846→under a namespace prefix, with coordinated specification, validation, and rendering components.
  1847→The specification defines what can be configured;
  1848→{axrg_assignment_s}
  1849→provide specific values.
  1850→
  1851→[[axrg_variable]]
  1852→{axrg_variable}::
  1853→A
  1854→{axl_motif}
  1855→representing a named configuration element at schema level.
  1856→Each
  1857→{axrg_variable}
  1858→belongs to an
  1859→{axrg_regime}
  1860→and defines a name, type constraints, and relationships with other variables.
  1861→{axrg_variable_s} are the "what can be configured" definitions;
  1862→{axrg_assignment_s}
  1863→bind values to them.
  1864→
  1865→[[axrg_assignment]]
  1866→{axrg_assignment}::
  1867→A
  1868→{axl_motif}
  1869→representing a specific value bound to an
  1870→{axrg_variable}.
  1871→{axrg_assignment_s} are carried in files of a particular
  1872→{axf_format}
  1873→(bash, makefile, JSON, etc.).
  1874→Multiple assignment files may exist for the same
  1875→{axrg_regime},
  1876→each providing different configuration instances.
  1877→
  1878→[[axrg_prefix]]
  1879→{axrg_prefix}::
  1880→A
  1881→{axl_motif}
  1882→representing the stable character sequence that namespaces all
  1883→{axrg_variable_s}
  1884→within an
  1885→{axrg_regime}.
  1886→Typically composed of a project marker and regime marker (e.g., `RBRR_` = `RB` + `RR` + `_`).
  1887→Once established, an
  1888→{axrg_prefix}
  1889→becomes deeply embedded across code, configuration files, and documentation.
  1890→Changing an
  1891→{axrg_prefix}
  1892→requires coordinated refactoring across all consuming artifacts.
  1893→
  1894→== Axial Regime Provenance Dimensions
  1895→
  1896→Provenance dimensions qualify
  1897→{axrg_assignment}
  1898→to declare how values arrive at
  1899→{axvr_broach}
  1900→time.
  1901→These are valid dimensions on
  1902→{axvr_regime}
  1903→declaring which provenance(s) a regime supports.
  1904→
  1905→[[axrd_file_sourced]]
  1906→{axrd_file_sourced}::
  1907→Assignments loaded from a persistent file via source or equivalent mechanism.
  1908→The file path is known at load time and the regime module is responsible for locating and sourcing it.
  1909→
  1910→[[axrd_env_inherited]]
  1911→{axrd_env_inherited}::
  1912→Assignments received as environment variables from a parent process.
  1913→The regime module finds variables already in scope; no file sourcing occurs.
  1914→
  1915→[[axrd_constructed]]
  1916→{axrd_constructed}::
  1917→Assignments computed or set programmatically before broach.
  1918→Values are assembled by application logic rather than loaded from a static source.
  1919→
  1920→== Axial Regime Annotation Terms
  1921→
  1922→Regime annotations use the prefix-discriminated form (`//ax...` with no space after `//`) to provide structured metadata about regime definitions in AsciiDoc specifications.
  1923→Two complementary annotation families enable regime specification across parent documents and their subdocuments.
  1924→
  1925→=== Definition-Site Voicing Annotations (axvr_*)
  1926→
  1927→{axvr_*} annotations appear in parent documents between anchor and definition, declaring the regime role of the term being defined.
  1928→The surface syntax follows the prefix-discriminated pattern:
  1929→
  1930→[source,asciidoc]
  1931→----
  1932→[[rbrv_sigil]]
  1933→//axvr_variable axd_required rbst_xname
  1934→{rbrv_sigil}::
  1935→Vessel identifier for build configurations.
  1936→----
  1937→
  1938→The annotation line starts with `//axvr_` (no space after `//`), followed by the specific voicing term, then space-separated dimension and type terms.
  1939→The anchor immediately preceding the annotation must match the first
  1940→{mcm_attribute_reference}
  1941→in the definition text that follows.
  1942→
  1943→[[axvr_regime]]
  1944→{axvr_regime}::
  1945→Voices a regime definition.
  1946→The annotated term defines a structured configuration system that binds related
  1947→{axrg_variable_s}
  1948→under a namespace prefix.
  1949→+
  1950→{axd_dimension_s}:
  1951→{axf_format}
  1952→(specifying how
  1953→{axrg_assignment_s}
  1954→are carried).
  1955→
  1956→[[axvr_variable]]
  1957→{axvr_variable}::
  1958→Voices a regime variable definition.
  1959→The annotated term defines a named configuration element within a regime.
  1960→The definition text must include, as its second
  1961→{mcm_attribute_reference},
  1962→the parent regime (an anchor that voices
  1963→{axrg_regime}).
  1964→This ties each variable unequivocally to its regime.
  1965→+
  1966→{axd_dimension_s}:
  1967→{axd_required}
  1968→or
  1969→{axd_optional},
  1970→type
  1971→{axl_motif}
  1972→(base type or type voicing).
  1973→
  1974→[[axvr_group]]
  1975→{axvr_group}::
  1976→Voices a regime group definition.
  1977→The annotated term defines a named grouping of related
  1978→{axrg_variable_s}
  1979→within a regime.
  1980→The definition text must include, as its second
  1981→{mcm_attribute_reference},
  1982→the parent regime (an anchor that voices
  1983→{axrg_regime}).
  1984→+
  1985→{axd_dimension_s}:
  1986→{axd_conditional}
  1987→(group activation depends on gate conditions in subdocument).
  1988→
  1989→[[axvr_broach]]
  1990→{axvr_broach}::
  1991→Voices a regime broach operation definition.
  1992→The annotated term defines the silent initialization phase for a
  1993→{axrg_regime}:
  1994→setting defaults, detecting unexpected variables, and building derived structures.
  1995→
  1996→[[axvr_validate]]
  1997→{axvr_validate}::
  1998→Voices a regime validate operation definition.
  1999→The annotated term defines the strict verification phase for a
  2000→{axrg_regime}:
  2001→checking all type and constraint requirements, failing on first violation.
  2002→
  2003→[[axvr_render]]
  2004→{axvr_render}::
  2005→Voices a regime render operation definition.
  2006→The annotated term defines the diagnostic display phase for a
  2007→{axrg_regime}:
  2008→producing human-readable presentation of current state.
  2009→Rendering occurs before validation so all fields are visible on failure.
  2010→
  2011→=== Standalone Hierarchy Markers (axhr*_)
  2012→
  2013→Hierarchy markers appear in regime subdocuments as standalone annotations, NOT between anchor and definition.
  2014→Each marker uses the prefix-discriminated form and reads ahead a specified number of
  2015→{mcm_attribute_reference_s}
  2016→from the following text to establish hierarchical context.
  2017→
  2018→The surface syntax:
  2019→
  2020→[source,asciidoc]
  2021→----
  2022→//axhrb_regime
  2023→{rbrv_regime}
  2024→
  2025→//axhrgv_variable
  2026→{rbrv_sigil}
  2027→----
  2028→
  2029→The hierarchy marker line starts with `//axhr` (no space after `//`), followed by the sub-prefix identifying the marker type.
  2030→Following text contains the
  2031→{mcm_attribute_reference_s}
  2032→that the marker reads ahead.
  2033→
  2034→==== Marker Terms and Lookahead Arities
  2035→
  2036→[cols="<,^,<",options="header"]
  2037→|===
  2038→|Marker |Lookahead |Reads
  2039→
  2040→|{axhrb_regime}     |1 {mcm_attribute_reference}  |The regime this subdoc describes
  2041→|{axhrv_variable}   |1 {mcm_attribute_reference}  |An ungrouped regime variable
  2042→|{axhrgb_group}     |1 {mcm_attribute_reference}  |A group (must match {axvr_group} in parent)
  2043→|{axhrgc_gate}      |2 {mcm_attribute_reference_s}|Enumerated variable + enum value
  2044→|{axhrgv_variable}  |1 {mcm_attribute_reference}  |A variable within current group
  2045→|{axhro_broach}     |1 {mcm_attribute_reference}  |Kit-level broach operation for this regime
  2046→|{axhro_validate}   |1 {mcm_attribute_reference}  |Kit-level validate operation for this regime
  2047→|{axhro_render}     |1 {mcm_attribute_reference}  |Kit-level render operation for this regime
  2048→|===
  2049→
  2050→[[axhrb_regime]]
  2051→{axhrb_regime}::
  2052→Marks the regime that a subdocument describes.
  2053→Lookahead: 1
  2054→{mcm_attribute_reference}
  2055→— the regime this subdoc describes.
  2056→The referenced attribute must correspond to an anchor annotated with
  2057→{axvr_regime}
  2058→in the parent document.
  2059→
  2060→[[axhrv_variable]]
  2061→{axhrv_variable}::
  2062→Marks an ungrouped regime variable in a subdocument.
  2063→Lookahead: 1
  2064→{mcm_attribute_reference}
  2065→— the variable being detailed.
  2066→
  2067→[[axhrgb_group]]
  2068→{axhrgb_group}::
  2069→Marks a group boundary within a subdocument.
  2070→Lookahead: 1
  2071→{mcm_attribute_reference}
  2072→— the group being detailed.
  2073→The referenced attribute must match an anchor annotated with
  2074→{axvr_group}
  2075→in the parent document.
  2076→
  2077→[[axhrgc_gate]]
  2078→{axhrgc_gate}::
  2079→Marks a conditional activation gate within a group.
  2080→Lookahead: 2
  2081→{mcm_attribute_reference_s}
  2082→— the enumerated variable (voicing
  2083→{axt_enumeration})
  2084→and the specific
  2085→{axt_enum_value}
  2086→that activates this gate.
  2087→The gate variable may belong to a different regime (cross-regime gating is legal).
  2088→
  2089→[[axhrgv_variable]]
  2090→{axhrgv_variable}::
  2091→Marks a variable within the current group in a subdocument.
  2092→Lookahead: 1
  2093→{mcm_attribute_reference}
  2094→— the variable being detailed within the current group scope.
  2095→
  2096→[[axhro_broach]]
  2097→{axhro_broach}::
  2098→Marks a regime broach operation description in a subdocument.
  2099→Lookahead: 1
  2100→{mcm_attribute_reference}
  2101→— the kit-level broach operation term (which must be voiced
  2102→{axvr_broach}
  2103→in the parent document).
  2104→
  2105→[[axhro_validate]]
  2106→{axhro_validate}::
  2107→Marks a regime validate operation description in a subdocument.
  2108→Lookahead: 1
  2109→{mcm_attribute_reference}
  2110→— the kit-level validate operation term (which must be voiced
  2111→{axvr_validate}
  2112→in the parent document).
  2113→
  2114→[[axhro_render]]
  2115→{axhro_render}::
  2116→Marks a regime render operation description in a subdocument.
  2117→Lookahead: 1
  2118→{mcm_attribute_reference}
  2119→— the kit-level render operation term (which must be voiced
  2120→{axvr_render}
  2121→in the parent document).
  2122→
  2123→==== Nesting Rules
  2124→
  2125→Hierarchy markers establish implicit scope through document order:
  2126→
  2127→* Higher-level markers implicitly close previous scope (no end markers needed)
  2128→* {axhrgb_group},
  2129→{axhrgc_gate},
  2130→and
  2131→{axhrgv_variable}
  2132→must appear within
  2133→{axhrb_regime}
  2134→scope
  2135→* {axhrgc_gate}
  2136→and
  2137→{axhrgv_variable}
  2138→must appear within
  2139→{axhrgb_group}
  2140→scope
  2141→* Multiple
  2142→{axhrgc_gate}
  2143→within one group have AND semantics
  2144→* {axhro_broach},
  2145→{axhro_validate},
  2146→and
  2147→{axhro_render}
  2148→must appear within
  2149→{axhrb_regime}
  2150→scope but outside any
  2151→{axhrgb_group}
  2152→scope
  2153→
  2154→==== Cross-Document Validation
  2155→
  2156→* {axhrb_regime}
  2157→attribute reference must match a regime defined with
  2158→{axvr_regime}
  2159→in parent
  2160→* {axhrgb_group}
  2161→attribute reference must match a group defined with
  2162→{axvr_group}
  2163→under that regime
  2164→* {axhrgc_gate}
  2165→first attribute reference must voice
  2166→{axt_enumeration};
  2167→second must be an
  2168→{axt_enum_value}
  2169→of that specific enumeration type
  2170→
  2171→== Axial Type Universal Terms
  2172→
  2173→{axtu_*} types represent generic infrastructure patterns that are not specific to any cloud provider or platform.
  2174→These types capture critical gestalts that appear across many systems.
  2175→Platform-specific nuances (e.g., path separators on Windows vs Unix) are handled through
  2176→{axl_voicing_s}.
  2177→
  2178→[[axtu_string]]
  2179→{axtu_string}::
  2180→A
  2181→{axl_motif}
  2182→representing a generic bounded string value.
  2183→{axl_voicing_s} specify minimum and maximum length constraints appropriate to their context.
  2184→
  2185→[[axtu_path]]
  2186→{axtu_path}::
  2187→A
  2188→{axl_motif}
  2189→representing a file system path.
  2190→Platform differences (path separators, root conventions) are handled by
  2191→{axl_voicing_s}.
  2192→May be absolute or relative depending on context.
  2193→
  2194→[[axtu_ipv4]]
  2195→{axtu_ipv4}::
  2196→A
  2197→{axl_motif}
  2198→representing an IPv4 address in dotted-decimal notation.
  2199→Format: four octets 0-255 separated by dots (e.g., `192.168.1.1`).
  2200→
  2201→[[axtu_cidr]]
  2202→{axtu_cidr}::
  2203→A
  2204→{axl_motif}
  2205→representing a CIDR block for network addressing.
  2206→Format: IPv4 address followed by slash and prefix length (e.g., `10.0.0.0/8`).
  2207→
  2208→[[axtu_port]]
  2209→{axtu_port}::
  2210→A
  2211→{axl_motif}
  2212→representing a network port number.
  2213→Valid range: 1-65535. Well-known ports are 1-1023.
  2214→
  2215→[[axtu_domain]]
  2216→{axtu_domain}::
  2217→A
  2218→{axl_motif}
  2219→representing a DNS domain name.
  2220→Must start and end with alphanumeric characters; may contain dots and hyphens.
  2221→
  2222→[[axtu_sha256]]
  2223→{axtu_sha256}::
  2224→A
  2225→{axl_motif}
  2226→representing a SHA-256 cryptographic hash.
  2227→Format: 64 lowercase hexadecimal characters.
  2228→
  2229→[[axtu_xname]]
  2230→{axtu_xname}::
  2231→A
  2232→{axl_motif}
  2233→representing a cross-context safe identifier.
  2234→Must start with a letter and contain only letters, digits, underscores, and hyphens.
  2235→Safe for use in file names, environment variables, and most programming contexts.
  2236→
  2237→== Axial Type Google Terms
  2238→
  2239→{axtg_*} types represent Google Cloud Platform resource identifiers and patterns.
  2240→These types have specific format requirements enforced by GCP APIs.
  2241→
  2242→[[axtg_project_id]]
  2243→{axtg_project_id}::
  2244→A
  2245→{axl_motif}
  2246→representing a Google Cloud Platform project identifier.
  2247→Format: 6-30 lowercase letters, digits, and hyphens; must start with a letter and end with letter or digit.
  2248→Globally unique across all of GCP.
  2249→
  2250→[[axtg_region]]
  2251→{axtg_region}::
  2252→A
  2253→{axl_motif}
  2254→representing a Google Cloud Platform region code.
  2255→Format: lowercase letters, digits, and hyphens (e.g., `us-central1`, `europe-west1`).
  2256→Determines geographic location for regional resources.
  2257→
  2258→[[axtg_service_account]]
  2259→{axtg_service_account}::
  2260→A
  2261→{axl_motif}
  2262→representing a Google Cloud Platform service account email.
  2263→Format: `{account-id}@{project-id}.iam.gserviceaccount.com` where account-id is 6-30 characters.
  2264→Used as identity for programmatic API access.
  2265→
  2266→[[axtg_billing_account]]
  2267→{axtg_billing_account}::
  2268→A
  2269→{axl_motif}
  2270→representing a Google Cloud Platform billing account identifier.
  2271→Format: `XXXXXX-XXXXXX-XXXXXX` (three groups of six uppercase alphanumeric characters).
  2272→Links projects to payment sources.
  2273→
  2274→[[axtg_project_number]]
  2275→{axtg_project_number}::
  2276→A
  2277→{axl_motif}
  2278→representing a Google Cloud Platform project number.
  2279→Format: numeric identifier automatically assigned at project creation.
  2280→Globally unique across all of GCP.
  2281→Immutable once assigned.
  2282→
  2283→== Axial Infrastructure Google Terms
  2284→
  2285→{axig_*} terms represent Google Cloud Platform infrastructure patterns.
  2286→Each term explicitly declares its level:
  2287→
  2288→* **Service-level**: the managed platform itself
  2289→* **Instance-level**: a particular resource within a service
  2290→* **Operational**: execution patterns and states
  2291→
  2292→=== Service-Level Terms
  2293→
  2294→[[axig_resource_manager]]
  2295→{axig_resource_manager}::
  2296→A
  2297→{axl_motif}
  2298→representing Google Cloud Resource Manager.
  2299→Service-level: the platform for managing organizations, folders, and projects.
  2300→
  2301→[[axig_cloud_storage]]
  2302→{axig_cloud_storage}::
  2303→A
  2304→{axl_motif}
  2305→representing Google Cloud Storage.
  2306→Service-level: the object storage platform, not buckets within it.
  2307→
  2308→[[axig_artifact_registry]]
  2309→{axig_artifact_registry}::
  2310→A
  2311→{axl_motif}
  2312→representing Google Artifact Registry.
  2313→Service-level: the artifact management platform, not repositories within it.
  2314→
  2315→[[axig_cloud_build]]
  2316→{axig_cloud_build}::
  2317→A
  2318→{axl_motif}
  2319→representing Google Cloud Build.
  2320→Service-level: the build execution platform, not individual builds.
  2321→
  2322→[[axig_iam]]
  2323→{axig_iam}::
  2324→A
  2325→{axl_motif}
  2326→representing Google Cloud IAM.
  2327→Service-level: the identity and access management platform.
  2328→
  2329→=== Instance-Level: Resource Manager
  2330→
  2331→[[axig_organization]]
  2332→{axig_organization}::
  2333→A
  2334→{axl_motif}
  2335→representing a Google Cloud organization.
  2336→Instance-level: a particular organization, top of GCP resource hierarchy.
  2337→
  2338→[[axig_folder]]
  2339→{axig_folder}::
  2340→A
  2341→{axl_motif}
  2342→representing a Google Cloud folder.
  2343→Instance-level: an organizational container within an
  2344→{axig_organization}.
  2345→
  2346→[[axig_project]]
  2347→{axig_project}::
  2348→A
  2349→{axl_motif}
  2350→representing a Google Cloud project.
  2351→Instance-level: a resource container with billing, APIs, and IAM.
  2352→
  2353→[[axig_lien]]
  2354→{axig_lien}::
  2355→A
  2356→{axl_motif}
  2357→representing a Google Cloud lien.
  2358→Instance-level: a deletion protection on a resource.
  2359→
  2360→=== Instance-Level: Cloud Storage
  2361→
  2362→[[axig_bucket]]
  2363→{axig_bucket}::
  2364→A
  2365→{axl_motif}
  2366→representing a Cloud Storage bucket.
  2367→Instance-level: an object container with ACLs and lifecycle policies.
  2368→
  2369→=== Instance-Level: Artifact Registry
  2370→
  2371→[[axig_artifact_repository]]
  2372→{axig_artifact_repository}::
  2373→A
  2374→{axl_motif}
  2375→representing an Artifact Registry repository.
  2376→Instance-level: container for container images or generic artifacts.
  2377→
  2378→[[axig_container_image]]
  2379→{axig_container_image}::
  2380→A
  2381→{axl_motif}
  2382→representing a container image in Artifact Registry.
  2383→Instance-level: an image with tags and digest.
  2384→
  2385→[[axig_generic_artifact]]
  2386→{axig_generic_artifact}::
  2387→A
  2388→{axl_motif}
  2389→representing a generic artifact in Artifact Registry.
  2390→Instance-level: a non-container artifact (archive, binary, metadata).
  2391→
  2392→=== Instance-Level: Cloud Build
  2393→
  2394→[[axig_build]]
  2395→{axig_build}::
  2396→A
  2397→{axl_motif}
  2398→representing a Cloud Build execution.
  2399→Instance-level: a particular build processing instructions to create artifacts.
  2400→
  2401→=== Instance-Level: IAM
  2402→
  2403→[[axig_service_account]]
  2404→{axig_service_account}::
  2405→A
  2406→{axl_motif}
  2407→representing a GCP service account.
  2408→Instance-level: a non-human identity for API authentication.
  2409→
  2410→[[axig_role]]
  2411→{axig_role}::
  2412→A
  2413→{axl_motif}
  2414→representing a GCP IAM role.
  2415→Instance-level: a named collection of permissions.
  2416→
  2417→[[axig_binding]]
  2418→{axig_binding}::
  2419→A
  2420→{axl_motif}
  2421→representing a GCP IAM policy binding.
  2422→Instance-level: association of identity to role on a resource scope.
  2423→
  2424→[[axig_service_account_key]]
  2425→{axig_service_account_key}::
  2426→A
  2427→{axl_motif}
  2428→representing a service account key file.
  2429→Instance-level: JSON credential for service account authentication.
  2430→
  2431→=== Operational Terms
  2432→
  2433→[[axig_lro]]
  2434→{axig_lro}::
  2435→A
  2436→{axl_motif}
  2437→representing a Long Running Operation.
  2438→Operational: async pattern returning handle for later completion check.
  2439→
  2440→[[axig_project_state]]
  2441→{axig_project_state}::
  2442→A
  2443→{axl_motif}
  2444→representing a project lifecycle state.
  2445→Operational: conditions like ACTIVE, DELETE_REQUESTED describing status.
  2446→
  2447→== Axial Key Premise Terms
  2448→
  2449→[[axk_premise]]
  2450→{axk_premise}::
  2451→A
  2452→{axl_motif}
  2453→representing a declared design constraint or assumption that explicitly bounds system complexity.
  2454→Premises eliminate classes of problems by stating what the system will not handle, simplifying design.
  2455→
  2456→== Infrastructure Terms
  2457→
  2458→[[axo_dependency]]
  2459→{axo_dependency}::
  2460→A
  2461→{axl_motif}
  2462→representing required component or library for operation.
  2463→Specifies external requirements for functionality.
  2464→
  2465→== Compliance Rules
  2466→
  2467→This section specifies compositional requirements between motifs.
  2468→When a term voices a particular motif, these rules define what companion elements are required.
  2469→
  2470→=== Procedure Documentation Completeness
  2471→
  2472→Voicing as
  2473→{axo_procedure}
  2474→requires documentation sections for:
  2475→
  2476→* {axs_inputs}
  2477→— what the procedure requires to execute
  2478→
  2479→* {axs_behavior}
  2480→— what the procedure does
  2481→
  2482→* {axs_outputs}
  2483→— what the procedure produces
  2484→
  2485→* {axs_completion}
  2486→— how the procedure signals success or failure
  2487→
  2488→Optional sections that MAY be included:
  2489→
  2490→* {axs_preconditions}
  2491→— environmental state requirements before execution
  2492→
  2493→* {axs_postconditions}
  2494→— environmental state guarantees after success
  2495→
  2496→* {axs_errors}
  2497→— failure modes and recovery guidance
  2498→
  2499→=== Command Documentation Completeness
  2500→
  2501→Voicing as
  2502→{axo_command}
  2503→requires all
  2504→{axo_procedure}
  2505→sections plus:
  2506→
  2507→* {axe_environment}
  2508→dimension specifying execution context (e.g.,
  2509→{axe_bash_interactive},
  2510→{axe_bash_unattended})
  2511→
  2512→=== Guide Documentation Completeness
  2513→
  2514→Voicing as
  2515→{axo_guide}
  2516→requires all
  2517→{axo_procedure}
  2518→sections plus:
  2519→
  2520→* {axs_behavior}
  2521→steps use
  2522→{axe_human_guide}
  2523→control voicings
  2524→
  2525→* Steps describe actions the human performs in external systems
  2526→
  2527→=== Lifecycle Dimension Requirement
  2528→
  2529→Every
  2530→{axo_procedure}
  2531→voicing requires exactly one lifecycle dimension:
  2532→
  2533→* {axd_transient}
  2534→— executes and completes (most common)
  2535→
  2536→* {axd_longrunning}
  2537→— runs until stopped
  2538→
  2539→* {axd_periodic}
  2540→— runs on schedule
  2541→
  2542→Lifecycle dimensions are mutually exclusive.
  2543→
  2544→=== Long-running Procedure Completeness
  2545→
  2546→Voicing with
  2547→{axd_longrunning}
  2548→requires all
  2549→{axo_procedure}
  2550→sections plus:
  2551→
  2552→* {axs_startup}
  2553→— initialization behavior and readiness criteria
  2554→
  2555→* {axs_shutdown}
  2556→— graceful termination and cleanup
  2557→
  2558→Optional sections:
  2559→
  2560→* {axs_health}
  2561→— liveness and readiness probes
  2562→
  2563→* {axs_signals}
  2564→— Unix signal handling
  2565→
  2566→* {axs_recovery}
  2567→— crash recovery behavior
  2568→
  2569→=== Periodic Procedure Completeness
  2570→
  2571→Voicing with
  2572→{axd_periodic}
  2573→requires all
  2574→{axo_procedure}
  2575→sections plus:
  2576→
  2577→* {axs_schedule}
  2578→— timing specification
  2579→
  2580→Optional sections:
  2581→
  2582→* {axs_idempotency}
  2583→— re-run safety guarantees
  2584→
  2585→* {axs_overlap}
  2586→— concurrent execution handling
  2587→
  2588→=== CLI Subcommand Completeness
  2589→
  2590→Voicing as
  2591→{axi_cli_subcommand}
  2592→requires:
  2593→
  2594→* Exit Status section voiced as
  2595→{axa_exit_uniform}
  2596→or
  2597→{axa_exit_enumerated}
  2598→(which voice
  2599→{axs_completion}
  2600→in CLI context)
  2601→
  2602→* Arguments section voiced as
  2603→{axa_argument_list}
  2604→(which voices
  2605→{axs_inputs}
  2606→in CLI context; may list zero arguments if operation takes none)
  2607→
  2608→=== CLI Option Completeness
  2609→
  2610→Voicing as
  2611→{axa_cli_option}
  2612→requires the definition text to specify:
  2613→
  2614→* Long form (e.g., `--file`)
  2615→
  2616→* Short form or explicit absence (e.g., `-f` or "none")
  2617→
  2618→* Default value, if the argument is used with
  2619→{axd_defaulted}
  2620→
  2621→=== Argument List Item Qualification
  2622→
  2623→Within an
  2624→{axa_argument_list},
  2625→each item requires a preceding
  2626→{axd_dimension}
  2627→annotation specifying at minimum:
  2628→
  2629→* {axd_required}
  2630→or
  2631→{axd_optional}
  2632→
  2633→* {axd_defaulted}
  2634→if a default value applies
  2635→
  2636→=== Type Voicing Qualification
  2637→
  2638→Voicing a type (subspecializing
  2639→{axt_*}
  2640→or
  2641→{axtu_*}
  2642→motifs) constrains dimension usage.
  2643→
  2644→Valid dimensions:
  2645→
  2646→* {axd_list}
  2647→— type intrinsically represents delimited collection
  2648→
  2649→Invalid dimensions:
  2650→
  2651→* {axd_required},
  2652→{axd_optional},
  2653→{axd_defaulted}
  2654→— presence applies to usage sites, not type definitions
  2655→
  2656→* {axd_repeated}
  2657→— repetition applies to usage sites, not type definitions
  2658→
  2659→=== Regime Specification Completeness
  2660→
  2661→Voicing as
  2662→{axrg_regime}
  2663→requires:
  2664→
  2665→* Format dimension (
  2666→{axf_bash},
  2667→{axf_makefile},
  2668→{axf_json},
  2669→etc.) specifying how assignments are carried
  2670→
  2671→* Provenance dimension (
  2672→{axrd_file_sourced},
  2673→{axrd_env_inherited},
  2674→or
  2675→{axrd_constructed})
  2676→declaring how
  2677→{axrg_assignment_s}
  2678→arrive
  2679→
  2680→=== Regime Variable Qualification
  2681→
  2682→Voicing as
  2683→{axrg_variable}
  2684→requires:
  2685→
  2686→* Type in annotation (base type motif or type voicing)
  2687→
  2688→* At minimum
  2689→{axd_required}
  2690→or
  2691→{axd_optional}
  2692→
  2693→* {axd_defaulted}
  2694→if a default value applies
  2695→
  2696→Valid dimensions:
  2697→
  2698→* {axd_required}
  2699→— variable must have assignment
  2700→
  2701→* {axd_optional}
  2702→— variable may be absent
  2703→
  2704→* {axd_defaulted}
  2705→— fallback value when absent
  2706→
  2707→* {axd_list}
  2708→— inherited from type voicing; variable's type is a delimited collection
  2709→
  2710→Invalid dimensions:
  2711→
  2712→* {axd_repeated}
  2713→— regime files use unique keys; multiple assignments of same variable not permitted
  2714→
  2715→* {axd_nullable}
  2716→— format-dependent;
  2717→{axf_bash}
  2718→has no null distinct from empty string
  2719→
  2720→=== Regime Voicing Annotation Completeness
  2721→
  2722→{axvr_regime}
  2723→requires:
  2724→
  2725→* {axf_format}
  2726→dimension specifying how
  2727→{axrg_assignment_s}
  2728→are carried
  2729→
  2730→{axvr_variable}
  2731→requires:
  2732→
  2733→* Second
  2734→{mcm_attribute_reference}
  2735→in definition text must be the parent regime
  2736→
  2737→* {axd_required}
  2738→or
  2739→{axd_optional}
  2740→
  2741→* Type
  2742→{axl_motif}
  2743→(base type or type voicing)
  2744→
  2745→{axvr_group}
  2746→requires:
  2747→
  2748→* Second
  2749→{mcm_attribute_reference}
  2750→in definition text must be the parent regime
  2751→
  2752→=== Regime Hierarchy Consistency
  2753→
  2754→* {axvr_group}
  2755→with
  2756→{axd_conditional}
  2757→requires corresponding
  2758→{axhrgb_group}
  2759→in subdocument to have at least one
  2760→{axhrgc_gate}
  2761→
  2762→* {axvr_group}
  2763→without
  2764→{axd_conditional}:
  2765→corresponding
  2766→{axhrgb_group}
  2767→must have no
  2768→{axhrgc_gate}
  2769→
  2770→* Every
  2771→{axvr_variable}
  2772→under a regime should appear as either
  2773→{axhrv_variable}
  2774→or
  2775→{axhrgv_variable}
  2776→in the subdocument
  2777→
